text:
Sold $CRDO commons & bought some LEAPS.

​Market action has been weird lately with spikes and drops. Feels like sector rotation or the start of a broader drawdown, so I'm playing it safe and slowly accumulating the beaten-down names on my watchlist.

****
​Sold:
$ZS commons (still holding LEAPS)

****
​Bought:
$KTOS (adding slowly)
$MDA (added more under $40)
$RDW (added more under $18)

****
​Missed the dip on these or do we get another look?
$GOOGL @ $355
$META @ $598
$AMZN @ $247
$CRWV @ $105 (speculative)
$CRDO @ $200 (to rebuy commons)
$AAOI @ $171
​Time will tell. Just putting my thoughts out there to keep myself honest. 

****
Quick note about $CRDO
Love $CRDO long term. They're already deeply integrated with major semiconductor players and hyperscalers. The DustPhotonics acquisition (closed late May 2026) is a big bullish catalyst.
With all the optics M&A happening lately, buying Dust is a smart power move. It brings silicon photonics tech in-house so they can offer a full vertical stack — SerDes, DSP, and optics.
That lets them deliver complete 800G and 1.6T end-to-end solutions right into their existing pipelines instead of just chips.
Optics alone expected to do >$600M in FY2027 (roughly double prior estimates), driving about half of their total revenue growth (>80% YoY). Accretive too.
Marvell $MRVL CEO Matt Murphy said in his Computex 2026 keynote that the copper wall is now moving inside the rack — CPO and optics are the future for AI scaling. CRDO looks well positioned. Massive growth driver for FY27 & beyond.

those are double consensus, which is reasonable tbh as the street is WAY behind since most estimates are actually based on pre dust photonics revenue. 
Even Existing hyper scalers will get it there I think. 

But additional business including SiPho PICs, optics and cxl will get to $20s. 

Maybe I’m the outlier being 3-4x consensus. I felt like that when I was forecasting $50 for MU in 2026. Which proved conservative oddly, despite being 3x above consensus.

text:
Interesting article from Swedish media..

Nvidia is investing $6.5 billion into photonics.

Why? Data centers consume too much power. The fix: move data with light instead of electricity.

This is exactly the market $SIVE operates in.

Nvidia isn’t building this alone, it needs component suppliers. Sivers is one of the few publicly listed InP photonics companies in the world.

• Coherent — $2B + purchase commitments
• Lumentum — $2B
• Marvell — $2B (silicon photonics)
• Corning — $500M
• Ayar Labs — $500M Series 

Wouldn’t be suprised seeing $NVDA funding $SIVE too at some point for CPO build-out

text:
Sivers $SIVEF $SIVE just landed exactly the validation the thesis needed.

Its laser arrays are now integrated into $GFS ’s silicon photonics + SCALE co-packaged optics platform -> the reference designs the AI data center buildout is being built on.

This is the “light, not copper” shift made concrete. The lasers that move data inside the cluster don’t get the headlines. They get the design wins.

Still early innings but the chokepoint is real, and $SIVE just got named in it. 🔥

text:
$GFS - GLOBALFOUNDRIES CLOSED TWO AI DEALS IN ONE DAY — PROCESSOR IP AND SILICON-PHOTONICS LASERS

On Tuesday GlobalFoundries completed its acquisition of Synopsys' ARC processor-IP business — which, bolted onto MIPS, gives it a software-to-silicon stack aimed at physical AI — and separately announced a silicon-photonics partnership with Sivers to integrate laser arrays into its AI-data-center optical reference designs. Shares rose ~6%.

GF is repositioning from a commodity trailing-edge foundry into the connectivity and edge-AI layers — processor IP, photonics, and a U.S.-backed quantum foundry — where the multiple expansion lives, on top of the first dividend it declared last month.

text:
🧠 $CRDO (Credo) — one of the strongest AI networking stocks nobody talked about two years ago

If $NVDA is the brain of AI,

then $CRDO is becoming one of the companies that builds the nervous system.

The company focuses on:

high-speed interconnects
Active Electrical Cables (AECs)
optical DSPs
silicon photonics
SerDes technology

for hyperscale AI data centers. (Credo)

⚙️ Where CRDO sits in the AI stack

AI Stack:

🧠 Compute

$NVDA

$AMD
💾 Memory

$MU

🌐 Networking

$AVGO

$MRVL
$CRDO
🔦 Optics

$LITE

$COHR
CRDO sits directly in:

AI connectivity and data movement.

As AI clusters scale toward hundreds of thousands or even millions of GPUs, the networking layer becomes increasingly critical. (Credo)

🔥 Why institutions are excited

1️⃣ Explosive growth

Recent fiscal Q4 results were enormous:

Revenue up 157% YoY

EPS up 231% YoY

Sixth consecutive quarter of triple-digit revenue growth
Yet the stock sold off because expectations had become extremely high. (Barron's)
2️⃣ AEC leadership

Credo is widely viewed as the leader in:

Active Electrical Cables (AECs)

These products are increasingly used by hyperscalers because they:

consume less power

improve reliability

support higher bandwidth AI clusters. (Credo)

Major hyperscalers are already customers. (Barron's)
3️⃣ Moving aggressively into optics
This is the part many investors miss.

Credo recently acquired DustPhotonics to bring silicon photonics technology in-house, expanding into:

800G optics

1.6T optics

future CPO architectures

This significantly strengthens its position in the next generation of AI networking. (MarketWatch)

🧠 Why CRDO matters for the CPO theme
You recently asked about Bank of America's CPO report.
The most interesting names were:

AVGO

MRVL

LITE

COHR

I would add:

CRDO is becoming a sleeper CPO beneficiary.
Because the company now spans:
copper interconnects
optical DSPs

silicon photonics

near-package optical connectivity

Management increasingly describes the company as a connectivity platform spanning copper, optics, telemetry, and future architectures. (Seeking Alpha)

⚔️ CRDO vs peers
$AVGO
networking king
lower risk

larger company

$MRVL

switch ASICs + custom AI silicon

$LITE

optical components
$CRDO
fastest growth

connectivity specialist

highest AI networking leverage

⚠️ Risks

🧊 Valuation

The stock has already had a huge run.

Expectations are extremely high. (Barron's)
🧊 Customer concentration
Large hyperscalers drive a significant portion of demand. (Chronicle Journal Markets)

🧊 AI capex cycle

If hyperscaler spending slows:

networking names can correct sharply.

🧠 My take

Among the AI networking names, my ranking today is:

$AVGO — safest leader

$CRDO — highest growth

$MRVL — strongest upside optionality

$LITE — optics leader

If your thesis is:

AI clusters continue getting bigger,

then networking may become as important as GPUs.

And CRDO is one of the purest ways to play that trend. (Credo)

Bottom line
$CRDO is no longer just a cable company.
It is becoming:
an AI connectivity platform spanning AECs, optics, silicon photonics, and next-generation AI networking.

For investors looking beyond GPUs, CRDO is one of the most important names to watch in the AI data movement layer. (Credo)

text:
Let me explain what the GlobalFoundries deal actually does for $SIVE — because most retail is reading "partnership" and missing the structural significance.

Reference designs are how foundry ecosystems work.
When a chip designer wants to build silicon photonics (let's say AMD, Broadcom, or a hyperscaler ASIC team), they don't start from scratch. They start with the foundry's reference design — a pre-validated blueprint for how the components fit together.
GlobalFoundries' silicon photonics platform is one of the most strategically positioned in the industry. Their SCALE™ optical engine is the foundation for next-generation co-packaged optics.

$SIVE's lasers are now embedded in that reference design.

What this means in practice:

→ Every customer who designs silicon photonics on GF's platform starts with $SIVE lasers as the default light source
→ Switching to a non-$SIVE laser requires custom engineering work, custom validation, and supply chain risk
→ $SIVE has effectively become the path of least resistance for the entire GF silicon photonics customer base

The math of why this is different from a normal customer win:
A typical customer deal = one company buys $SIVE lasers.

A foundry reference design = every customer using that foundry's silicon photonics platform encounters $SIVE lasers in their default design flow.

One captures one customer. The other captures an ecosystem.

The competitive context:
Coherent, Lumentum, MACOM — these are the larger optical/photonics names retail compares $SIVE to. None of them just got embedded in a major foundry's silicon photonics reference design at the laser layer.

$SIVE just did something its bigger competitors did not.
And the addressable market GF is targeting? $25B in pluggable optics by 2030.

This is the moat people argued $SIVE didn't have. It just got built. $SIVE

1,200 Active Screens and Generating Fourth Quarter Adjusted EBITDA of $3.2 Million Fueled by $3.0 Million of Royalties “D-BOX delivered exceptional fourth quarter and fiscal 2026 results, reflecting continued execution across our theatrical focused growth strategy” said Naveen Prasad, CEO of D-BOX. “During the year, we achieved a record expansion of our theatrical footprint by adding 189 net new screens, taking us to 1,201 active theatrical screens globally. This growth reflects increasing recognition from our exhibitor partners that D-BOX is a highly deployable premium experience that can help maximize per-patron yield by delivering a uniquely immersive experience for audiences of all ages. Our total revenues grew by 35% year-over-year to $57.6 million, while adjusted EBITDA more than doubled to $15.5 million. Net profit before income taxes surged 193% to $11.4 million. These robust results have further strengthened our balance sheet and enhanced our financial flexibility, positioning D-BOX to offer innovative financing solutions and capitalize on strategic opportunities as we move forward.” Q4 2026 Operating Results Fourth quarter royalty revenues increased 33% to $3.0 million compared with $2.2 million in the prior year. This year‑over‑year increase was driven primarily by a 24.8%1 increase in the North American domestic box office, and the 18.7% expansion in our installed footprint. For the second consecutive quarter, the gross domestic box office remained less concentrated across a broader range of films, reflective of the seasonality and composition of the release calendar during the period, rather than being dominated by blockbuster titles, which are typically more favorable for D-BOX. D-BOX-encoded movies themselves delivered strong results among the highest grossing titles of the quarter, including Project Hail Mary, Avatar: Fire and Ash and Hoppers. System sales to theatrical customers increased 682% to $7.8 million compared with $1.0 million last year. This increase reflects D-BOX’s emergence as a preferred capital allocation priority for it’s exhibitors partners looking to maximize per-patron yield. Seventy four new screens were installed in the quarter, offset by 8 deactivations, compared to 6 net new screens from the same period last year. While this surge highlights the D-BOX brand’s growing market penetration and the impact of high-performing blockbuster content, we continue to maintain a disciplined outlook regarding future hardware cycles. We expect system sales to normalize toward historical patterns of seasonality, as exhibitor capital expenditures typically fluctuate based on our exhibitor partners' positioning for the strength and timing of the global box office slate and their corresponding free cash flow generation. The Company ended the year with a significantly improved financial position: cash and cash equivalents increased to $17.6 million (up from $7.8 million), working capital rose to $24.7 million, and equity nearly doubled to $34.6 million. These improvements materially strengthen the Company's financial flexibility provide a solid foundation for continued investing in innovation, operational excellence, and strategic exhibitor financing and deployment initiatives. The strengthened balance sheet also supports disciplined capital allocation initiatives focused on creating long term shareholder value, including initiatives such as the Normal Course Issuer Bid (NCIB). Additionally, the Company recorded a deferred tax asset (DTA) of $6.4 million, relating to the recognition of previously unused tax losses and unused tax credits. This recognition is based on the expectation that future taxable profits will be available to utilize these losses and credits, supported by the Company’s recent history of success and positive outlook for future cash flows and profitability. Net profit for the quarter reached $1.9 million. Adjusted EBITDA2 totaled $3.2 million, representing a 22% Adjusted EBITDA margin², up 105% year-over-year and demonstrating continued focus on cost control and operational efficiency. Given the inherent variability and seasonality of quarterly sales, we continue to emphasize the importance of assessing the Company’s performance on a trailing twelve-month basis. FY26 Operating Results Total revenues increased by 35% year-over-year to $57.6 million, with adjusted EBITDA more than doubling to $15.5 million and net profit before income taxes rising 193% to $11.4 million. The Company’s strong top-line growth was driven primarily by a surge in theatrical system sales (up 133% for the year) and recurring rights for use, rental, and maintenance revenues (up 32%). This performance underscores the effectiveness of D-BOX’s commercial strategy and its ability to scale profitably. D-BOX continued to outperform the North American domestic box office on an annualized basis, with the box office increasing 7%3 while D-BOX royalties rose 32%. Our base of motion-enabled screens grew by 18.7% year-over-year, reaching 1,201 active screens. D-BOX’s technology is increasingly recognized as a premium upgrade by exhibitors, enabling them to maximize per-patron yield and deliver unique, immersive experiences. The expansion of the installed footprint directly supports high-margin, recurring royalty and maintenance revenues, which have proven resilient and outperformed broader box office trends. This continued outperformance reinforces D-BOX’s growing position within the premium theatrical ecosystem and further supports the long-term scalability of its recurring revenue model. Although revenues from sim racing and simulation and training system sales declined by 10% and 16% respectively, these segments continue to play a strategically important role within D-BOX’s broader commercial platform and overall profitability profile. Both customer groups utilize the same core motion technology and components as our theatrical clients, which allows D-BOX to leverage economies of scale across its operations. By sharing operating overhead and production resources, these segments help optimize cost efficiency and enhance profitability, even during periods of lower sales. This synergy not only mitigates risk but also improves operating leverage, enhances production scale efficiencies, and preserves D-BOX’s ability to capitalize on growth opportunities across multiple end markets."}}}, "rest_id": "2061921009972744328", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "4088", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2061924590696185880", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2526", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Portfolio Update (June 2026): +158.57% (YTD)

1. $SIVE (40%) - CPO / 1.6T for Photonics

I didn’t touch $SIVE this month. I held my position and let the market do the work. It was a 30% allocation, but after a 75% surge over the last 30 days, it grew to 40%.

The thesis is simple, and my conviction has only deepened over the last few months. People are cheering for $10, but they are missing the bigger picture: we haven’t even seen what 2027 looks like yet. Or 2028! 

There is already structural validation across the board with hyperscalers, OEMs, $AAPL, $AEVA, and US defense supply chains. Add factors like the institutional reports, $GFS tech validation, the upcoming US listing, Chips Act, MOU... 

Almost lost count of how many catalysts we got. 😅

$NVDA needs 20x more InP laser capacity by 2030, and current suppliers can only address 60% of that. Guess what? $SIVE comes to the rescue!

Will look to keep accumulating during decent entries throughout H2'26. 

2. $NBIS (35%) - AI Cloud for Compute

Up from 30% to 35% of my portfolio. This is a long-term compounder. The compute demand is astronomical, and their world-class technology and software integration set them apart. 

Management completely won me over. I see this as a $100B company by year-end. If anyone is positioned to become the 4th major hyperscaler, it is Nebius. 

With major catalysts lined up for H2 2026, selling - or even trimming - would be absolutely criminal in my view.

Also it's Jensen's favorite child. 😆

3. $SMCI & $PENG (10% overall) - AI Servers

Both ride the massive AI server trend (heading to $100 soon), but with distinct technological edges.

$PENG caught my eye because of its immense upside in the memory and photonics sectors, which will take their margins to a completely new level compared to today.

$SMCI is setting up for a $DELL like run. Trading at less than 1x sales? It is a no-brainer. AI server demand is compounding, and only 40% of planned US data centers are currently operational. Once the export-control narrative fades, the upside is massive - especially with Elon (SpaceX, xAI, Tesla) as a client. 

4. $TRT (5%) - Back-end Testing

ATMP is still an underlooked sector in the AI infrastructure buildout with many hidden goldmines. 

$TRT looks great to capitalize on the critical need for chip testing across multiple sectors. When demand vastly outstrips supply, the margin for low-quality chips shrinks to zero.

They are posting triple-digit growth, and margins will aggressively expand through 2027 and 2028 as new orders flood in. 

The current upside is driven by CPU/GPUs (with $AMD as the likely customer) and Power Semi, but I see them expanding into Memory and Photonics as well. 

Capacity expansion is a massive bullish signal, and their balance sheet is pristine (0.1 D/E). 

I see this easily breaking $50 by the end of the year, or H1 2027 at the latest.

5. $AAOI & $MU (5%)

$MU is a multi-month swing trade for me, riding the road to a $2T/$3T valuation backed by the American Dream memory narrative.

With $AAOI, I am still evaluating. I might swing it to $300, or I might accumulate more as a long position, because this definitely looks like a $750 stock by 2028. 

Both are tied to massive, sector-specific demand ramps, with $AAOI in transceiver sales and $MU with HBM. 

6. Cash (5%)

I am actually proud of maintaining a decent cash position. Had I not bought $MU, $AAOI, $SMCI, it would have been around 10%. 

This is giving me the optionality to buy the dip on my core holdings if they hit key levels, or to execute new swing setups when I see the right entry.

Things I did this month:

> Sold $KRKNF & $ZETA: Exited these positions to reallocate into higher-conviction plays within the core AI Infrastructure theme. Both remain great longs in my view, but this was purely a pivot for better short-term upside.

> Locked in some massive gains on $SOI, $AAOI, and $TSEM. However, I ended up getting back into $AAOI after the market handed me a $150 entry - an absolute gift.

> $SOI, $AAOI and $TSEM had also given me huge gains and so I decided to sell them. Bought $AAOI again because of the $150 entry, which was a gift. 

Looking ahead: 

> Now that I got memory exposure (last month's goal), my research is shifting toward Energy and Power Semi names. Looking to potentially add one or two high-conviction companies here next.

> I’m intentionally sitting out of Space stocks for now. While I wanted exposure last month, spreading myself too thin risks me loosing focus. Definitely don't want that. 

Make sure you keep doing your own research and building conviction. We already have many soft hands in this market. You don't need to be another one!

- AI

Photonics I didn’t touch $SIVE this month. I held my position and let the market do the work. It was a 30% allocation, but after a 75% surge over the last 30 days, it grew to 40%. The thesis is simple, and my conviction has only deepened over the last few months. People are cheering for $10, but they are missing the bigger picture: we haven’t even seen what 2027 looks like yet. Or 2028! There is already structural validation across the board with hyperscalers, OEMs, $AAPL, $AEVA, and US defense supply chains. Add factors like the institutional reports, $GFS tech validation, the upcoming US listing, Chips Act, MOU... Almost lost count of how many catalysts we got. 😅 $NVDA needs 20x more InP laser capacity by 2030, and current suppliers can only address 60% of that. Guess what? $SIVE comes to the rescue! Will look to keep accumulating during decent entries throughout H2'26. 2. $NBIS (35%) - AI Cloud for Compute Up from 30% to 35% of my portfolio. This is a long-term compounder. The compute demand is astronomical, and their world-class technology and software integration set them apart. Management completely won me over. I see this as a $100B company by year-end. If anyone is positioned to become the 4th major hyperscaler, it is Nebius. With major catalysts lined up for H2 2026, selling - or even trimming - would be absolutely criminal in my view. Also it's Jensen's favorite child. 😆 3. $SMCI & $PENG (10% overall) - AI Servers Both ride the massive AI server trend (heading to $100 soon), but with distinct technological edges. $PENG caught my eye because of its immense upside in the memory and photonics sectors, which will take their margins to a completely new level compared to today. $SMCI is setting up for a $DELL like run. Trading at less than 1x sales? It is a no-brainer. AI server demand is compounding, and only 40% of planned US data centers are currently operational. Once the export-control narrative fades, the upside is massive - especially with Elon (SpaceX, xAI, Tesla) as a client. 4. $TRT (5%) - Back-end Testing ATMP is still an underlooked sector in the AI infrastructure buildout with many hidden goldmines. $TRT looks great to capitalize on the critical need for chip testing across multiple sectors. When demand vastly outstrips supply, the margin for low-quality chips shrinks to zero. They are posting triple-digit growth, and margins will aggressively expand through 2027 and 2028 as new orders flood in. The current upside is driven by CPU/GPUs (with $AMD as the likely customer) and Power Semi, but I see them expanding into Memory and Photonics as well. Capacity expansion is a massive bullish signal, and their balance sheet is pristine (0.1 D/E). I see this easily breaking $50 by the end of the year, or H1 2027 at the latest. 5. $AAOI & $MU (5%) $MU is a multi-month swing trade for me, riding the road to a $2T/$3T valuation backed by the American Dream memory narrative. With $AAOI, I am still evaluating. I might swing it to $300, or I might accumulate more as a long position, because this definitely looks like a $750 stock by 2028. Both are tied to massive, sector-specific demand ramps, with $AAOI in transceiver sales and $MU with HBM. 6. Cash (5%) I am actually proud of maintaining a decent cash position. Had I not bought $MU, $AAOI, $SMCI, it would have been around 10%. This is giving me the optionality to buy the dip on my core holdings if they hit key levels, or to execute new swing setups when I see the right entry. Things I did this month: > Sold $KRKNF & $ZETA: Exited these positions to reallocate into higher-conviction plays within the core AI Infrastructure theme. Both remain great longs in my view, but this was purely a pivot for better short-term upside. > Locked in some massive gains on $SOI, $AAOI, and $TSEM. However, I ended up getting back into $AAOI after the market handed me a $150 entry - an absolute gift. > $SOI, $AAOI and $TSEM had also given me huge gains and so I decided to sell them. Bought $AAOI again because of the $150 entry, which was a gift. Looking ahead: > Now that I got memory exposure (last month's goal), my research is shifting toward Energy and Power Semi names. Looking to potentially add one or two high-conviction companies here next. > I’m intentionally sitting out of Space stocks for now. While I wanted exposure last month, spreading myself too thin risks me loosing focus. Definitely don't want that. Make sure you keep doing your own research and building conviction. We already have many soft hands in this market. You don't need to be another one! - AI"}}}, "rest_id": "2061923049142886527", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "75822", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

$ABNB (Airbnb) and $ALMU (Aeluma) delivered standout performances today.
$ABNB: Driven by new summer features and the 2026 World Cup, Q1 revenue rose 18% and guidance was raised; a leader in the travel recovery.
ALMU: Breakthrough in 300mm photonics platform and accelerated government quantum contracts; an early-stage commercialization play in AI and quantum computing.
Advantages: Combines consumer recovery with cutting-edge technology; low valuation with significant growth potential.
Should we continue monitoring: Yes, with multiple near-term catalysts and substantial mid-to-long-term potential.

text:
In addition to the smart ring, new ATMs and payment terminals, and a live stand, Sber presented several other new developments at the SPIEF.

One of these is GigaNetwork. This is a platform where AI agents autonomously perform routine business processes. They search for counterparties, conduct tenders, and support transactions at all stages.

An optical computer for AI tasks was also presented. This is a new type of device: it uses light, not electronics, for calculations. According to the developers, the transition to photonics will reduce the costs of training and using large models. This is a real way to build an efficient computing infrastructure.

text:
Is it weird to say I still feel bullish despite the massive collapse today? 6/9 stocks in my port are down over 10% today, with SK hynix taking the questionable throne, dropping a staggering 18.6%. This is the worst day for my port in 2026 so far.

Despite that, I still feel bullish, because $META is reportedly planning an equity issuance worth $10's of billions of dollars (per FT), following $GOOG's equity issuance earlier this week. This would mean even more CapEx spending, thus even more spending on memory and photonics. 

That is bullish for $MU, $SNDK, SK hynix and $AAOI in my port. For now, the charts look extremely ugly. Longer term, I remain bullish.

text:
$LITE $COHR $AAOI 

I’m obviously a huge Nvidia bull.

I’m a huge quantum bull.

I’m a huge memory bull.

And I’m a huge physical AI / Tesla bull.

Having said that, there is one category that stands out as potentially offering the best generational buying opportunity b/t now and the end of this correction. It is AI networking / optical (photonics) interconnects.

This category is poised to face a tighter supply bottleneck than chips and even memory during the next phase of AI infrastructure buildout. Personally, I’m already building my photonics portfolio w/ a 2 year holding horizon. Note that this is distinct from my quantum positions which I plan to hold for many more years.

text:
🚨 CONFIRMED via Finansinspektionen (Swedish FSA): JPMorgan Chase & Co. just crossed the 5% ownership threshold in $SIVE.

Official filing:
→ Holder: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
→ Transaction date: June 2, 2026 (same day as the GlobalFoundries announcement)
→ Reason for flagging: "Köp" (BUY)
→ Pre-transaction position: 0 shares
→ Post-transaction position: 16,098,583 shares / 5.25008% of total voting rights

Position structure:
→ J.P. Morgan Securities plc (UK entity): 15,916,852 voting rights — 5.19%
→ J.P. Morgan Securities LLC (US entity): 181,731 voting rights — 0.06%
→ Direct shares: 10,049,558 (3.14% of total shares)
→ Cash-settled instruments (likely swaps): 6,049,025 share equivalents

Why this is structurally significant:

Swedish law requires mandatory disclosure when crossing the 5% ownership threshold. JPMorgan went from ZERO to 5.25% on June 2. The flagging reason filed with the FSA was explicitly "Köp" (Buy) — not custody, not market-making inventory. This is a deliberate institutional position.

The timing is what makes it remarkable:

June 2 was the same day GlobalFoundries announced the silicon photonics reference design partnership and $SIVE moved +50-70%.

Three possible reads:
JPM was positioned beforehand and captured the move

JPM built the position aggressively into and after the news.

JPM is positioning ahead of the Nasdaq NY listing in an underwriter / placement agent role

All three are bullish. The third would be the most strategically significant — tier-1 US investment banks don't take 5% positions in Swedish micro-caps without strategic context.

Connecting back to the "zero institutional ownership" post earlier this week:

Five days ago: $SIVE had zero US institutional 13F filings.

Today: the world's largest investment bank discloses a 5%+ position triggered by a "Buy" transaction.

The institutional layer that was missing has started forming.
The composition tells you it's sophisticated:
6 million of the 16 million share-equivalents are in cash-settled derivatives. The rest is direct shares. This is the kind of structured position you set up when you're a major capital markets player preparing for a multi-event sequence — index inclusions, dual listing, sector rotation.

JPMorgan doesn't take 5% positions in $2-3B Swedish micro-caps because they're bored.

$SIVE just got its first major Wall Street institutional anchor.

$SIVE

text:
Quantum computing just went multi-platform and it’s mind blowing.  2026 isn’t about one winner taking all. Google just announced it’s adding neutral-atom systems alongside its superconducting roadmap. Companies are mixing neutral atoms, photonics, diamond spins, and more creating hybrid powerhouses.  
The era of “my qubits vs yours” is over. It’s now about smart combinations that actually deliver useful advantage. Let’s unpack why this feels like the real tipping point.

$TSM

text:
Aggregate inference compute demand grows regardless of where inference runs (Jevons paradox), which is the core of the bull case for hardware. 

Inference is structurally more open than training: Less CUDA-locked, more price sensitive. Regardless, I think enterprise will forever rely on neoclouds to reduce hardware CAPEX + in-house teams, so overarching investment thesis (whether big AI or locally hosted) is still the same. 

Stacked silicon, Photonics, Power Grids etc as y'all have been doing DD.

text:
-> IP acquisition
-> Just waiting for CPO to take off

$SIVE is a laser chokepoint for photonics and are publicly validated by:

- $GFS (1 of 2 public laser suppliers with $LITE for CPO per presentation)

- $JBL (“Relatively dramatic moat” for pluggables built with Sivers)

- CHIPS ACT for the overall company

For highest visibility:

-> Ayar is the largest CPO player that primary sources $SIVE and are expected to ramp in 2027.

-> $POET is another near term CPO volume player that’s heavily visible with Sivers.

For OSINT mapping:

$MRVL Celestial (direct, not through Poet), Lightmatter, Lightelligence, were all high confidence customers of Sivers. 

$SIVE is also developing/qualifying with multiple more optical transceiver players following Jabil.

It feels like they’re going to end up everywhere. 

I’m not sure people realize how special that many qualifications is coming from a <$2B MC laser company is… right before 2027 volume ramp. 

Especially while all the other laser companies trade at $15B-$70B valuations. 

Just need pluggables to bridge revenue gap into H2 2027 (CPO Scale up)

Then making every laser they sell more valuable following the $LITE playbook, to capture more TAM of both markets.  

- In the overarching optical $141B TAM (10x) in the next 1 1/2 years. (Goldman Sachs)

- and CPO TAM going from 0 to $81B in the next 1 1/2 years. 

So, easily multiply revenue opportunity overnight doing IP acquisition downstream. 

It’s more of just a waiting game, I think $SIVE is very undervalued relative to forward revenue potential. 

If it were a private Silicon Valley startup it would probably be worth $4-6B today.

Just needs to get listed on NASDAQ for premiums to bridge that gap.

text:
$SIVE CEO: “In the last 4-5 months we have received a lot of photonics opportunities to the level where both businesses (wireless and photonics) are challenging each other in terms of pipeline they occupy.”

In the past it was more focused on wireless. Now it isn’t. 👀

He also states that pipeline opportunities are at different stages. That’s why they are key to future revenue potential, as they are closely tied to execution - not just open numbers without a real path to production. 😉

Wall Street is still treating $STM like a legacy, cyclical automotive chipmaker. That narrative is completely broken. Buried beneath short-term restructuring costs and an elevated trailing multiple is a massive structural pivot into AI silicon photonics—quietly anchored by a newly disclosed warrant agreement with Amazon. While the market fixates on temporary factory underutilization, it is entirely missing a multi-billion dollar datacenter expansion. Here is the real investment thesis. 🌐🔬 #Semiconductors

text:
What’s so impressive to me about @joinautopilot’s “Photonics is Next” basket is that @michaelsikand + @KawzInvests & the folks at Autopilot were months ahead of the ETF wrappers now chasing the theme. $LYTE Photonics ETF from @roundhill is not yet listed, and $FOTO Photonics ETF just listed on May 29th. 

Before either existed, @joinautopilot provided the best pure-play photonics ETF exposure you could actually get. $EUV is the closest listed alternative, but it’s not a true pure play. It’s thematically diluted with semicap and foundry names like $TSM, $ASML, etc. 

By the time $LYTE finally lists, a lot of the alpha will likely already be gone. The growth is happening before the ETF shows up, not after.
Check $LITE & $AAOI YTD gain for reference.

Autopilot has proved it’s a serious platform for retail investors, not only a Congress copy-trading app everyone knows it for. Haven’t ever used Autopilot before, but I’m now open to the possibility. 

Bravo, @joinautopilot @michaelsikand @KawzInvests 🫰🫰🫰

(Appreciation post, not a paid promo)

text:
$sive CEO 인터뷰

1. 1분기 실적

- 환율과 미국 정부 셧다운 여파(정부 자금 및 국방 지출 승인 지연)로 인해 자체 목표보다 다소 아쉬운 실적을 기록함.

2. 사업 현황

- 현재 성장을 이끄는 무선(Wireless) 부문은 위성 통신 시장 확대를 준비 중이며, 미래 동력인 포토닉스(Photonics) 부문은 2027년 AI 데이터 센터 시장 진입을 준비하고 있음. 

3. 미국 상장 추진

- 미국 증시 교차 상장을 준비 중
- 규제와 비용 부담이 늘지만, 대규모 전문 투자자 확보와 장기적 자금 조달에 유리하며 이를 위해 재무 통제와 현장 인력 투자를 늘렸음.

4. 최종 목표

- 다수의 신제품이 본격적으로 대량 양산되는 2027년을 제품 폭발 성장의 해로 만드는 것이 현재 최우선 과제임.

text:
I've been looking into ZBLAN optical fiber as it's one of the clearer cases for manufacturing in space. 

ZBLAN is a heavy metal fluoride glass fiber. It offers the potential for lower optical loss than conventional silica fiber and transmits across a broader wavelength range into the mid-infrared. These traits could support longer transmission distances and new uses in sensing and communications.

On Earth, producing high-quality ZBLAN has been difficult. Gravity induces convection in the molten glass during the drawing process leads to microscopic crystals and defects that scatter light and limit performance.

In microgravity, these issues are reduced. The glass stays more homogeneous during drawing. Recent work by Flawless Photonics on the International Space Station produced over 11 kilometers of fiber in about a month.

The returned samples are under analysis, and microscope comparisons show much cleaner microstructures than Earth-produced fiber.

Challenges remain. ZBLAN is more brittle than silica and has a larger minimum bend radius, which affects handling. The technology is still in the demonstration phase but commercial production is being planned,and the plan is to manufacture it in space.

text:
🚨 Scientists shrank a powerful femtosecond laser onto a chip.

This is not a laser pointer upgrade. It is a photonics platform breakthrough.

Ultrafast lasers produce extremely short pulses of light, often used for precision measurement, spectroscopy, medical imaging, atomic clocks, and chemical analysis.

The problem:

Traditional ultrafast laser systems can be bulky and complex.

In a Nature paper, researchers demonstrated an integrated mode-locked laser using an erbium-implanted silicon nitride photonic chip and a Mamyshev oscillator design.

The key result:
The chip-scale laser produced nanojoule-level pulses, could be compressed to 147 femtoseconds, and directly drove a broad supercontinuum without extra amplification.

Why it matters:
This could help move powerful ultrafast laser tools from large lab systems toward compact, portable devices.

Important nuance:
Not a consumer product yet.
Not replacing every laser system tomorrow.

Still advanced photonics research.

Translation:
Real chip-scale laser milestone. Smaller tools.
Big potential for sensing, spectroscopy, medical diagnostics, and precision timing.

Source:
High-pulse-energy integrated mode-locked laser using a Mamyshev oscillator. Nature. 2026.
https://t.co/TE9fxK75U4

Follow @TheSynapseX for anti-hype, source-based science and technology corrections.

And people are extremely impatient now. Tech, semi's, photonics, optics, AI infra and Neo clouds, energy and memory are the play.

People can complain all they want. It's not stopping. Maybe a short term rise in software, financials and healthcare so tech can cool off, but you better believe "if that even happens" everyone will sell visa and united healthcare in 1 second for tech.

4007}, {"from_index": 4787, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4792}, {"from_index": 4831, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4847}]}, "text": "Copper can't keep up with AI. That's not an opinion, it's physics. Every data center being built right now is replacing electrical connections with light. NVIDIA confirmed it with $4.5 billion in direct investment. I mapped 25 public companies across the photonics value chain: Every AI cluster being built today hits the same wall. A hundred thousand GPUs mean nothing if the data can't move between them fast enough. Copper maxed out years ago and photonics replaced it: lasers, optical fiber, and transceivers that push data at the speed of light. The AI transceiver market doubled in two years. NVIDIA committed $4.5 billion across three photonics companies this year alone. This is where the infrastructure money is going. Here's the full value chain: 🔬 MATERIALS & WAFERS This is the bottom of the chain. Every laser and transceiver starts as a wafer substrate: indium phosphide, gallium arsenide, germanium, specialty glass. Nobody above this layer can produce anything without these inputs, and right now the most critical one, indium phosphide, is the tightest material in the entire AI supply chain. The gap between demand and capacity is getting worse, not better. I think this is the most asymmetric layer on the map. Investors chase the transceiver companies and ignore who grows the substrates underneath them. But NVIDIA is writing checks worth billions in cash and warrants to lock up supply from this exact layer. First link in the chain, last to get attention, and the one that chokes everything above it if it breaks. Tickers: $GLW, $AXTI, $IQE, $AIXA, $AMS 💡 CORE PHOTONIC DEVICES This layer converts electricity into light and back. Without it, zero data moves through fiber. NVIDIA dropped $4 billion into two companies here this year just to secure production capacity, and both of them joined the S&P 500 within weeks of each other. That should tell you how fast this went from niche to essential. The supply gap is not closing. The companies shipping next gen lasers at volume can be counted on one hand, and switching suppliers takes years of requalification. Order books stretch past twelve months. Every next generation GPU cluster consumes more of these components than the last, and no one can substitute them on short notice. I watch this layer more closely than any other. Tickers: $IPGP, $COHR, $LITE, $LASR, $SIVE 🔌 COMPONENTS & MODULES The companies here take raw lasers and detectors, package them into finished transceivers and modules, and ship them straight to hyperscalers. If the layers below are the engine, this is the vehicle that actually reaches the customer. Hyperscaler purchase orders land here. The revenue acceleration shows up here first. What I like about this layer is that you can underwrite it today, not in two years. These are businesses with signed capacity commitments and product already moving. The consolidation angle matters too: larger photonics players have already started absorbing standalone module companies, and whoever remains independent gains pricing power as options thin out. Tickers: $AAOI, $MTSI, $VIAV, $LPTH ⚙️ SYSTEMS & EQUIPMENT No company above this layer can manufacture a single photonic component without the machines built here. One of these names holds 100% of the EUV lithography market with zero competitors. Others supply the bonding equipment for co packaged optics or the process control instruments used across the majority of advanced packaging lines. If photonics is the gold rush, this is the layer selling the picks. My honest take: this is where the smart, patient capital parks. Equipment companies have pricing power and multi year order books that generate cash through full capex cycles. They attract holders who don't panic on the first pullback. The stocks don't run 1,000% overnight, but they compound while everything above them swings, and that tradeoff is worth more than most people give it credit for. Tickers: $ASML, $BESI, $ASM, $LPKF, $MKSI 🔍 TEST, METROLOGY & YIELD The most ignored layer on this map, and arguably the one with the cleanest business model. Every wafer, laser, and transceiver has to be tested and verified before it ships. As speeds climb and photonic devices get more complex, the testing challenge compounds fast. The industry is now constrained not only by what it can build but by what it can prove actually works. Yield is money. Better defect detection means better margins for every company upstream, which is why foundries keep buying test equipment even when they slash budgets everywhere else. These are capital light businesses tied to every unit of production across the chain. Last check before product hits the customer, and one of the few layers where demand doesn't cycle down when the rest of semis softens. Tickers: $CAMT, $FORM, $AEHR, $ONTO, $VIAV 🧠FINAL THOUGHTS The NVIDIA capital concentration tells the whole story. One company wrote $4.5 billion in checks to three photonics suppliers in a single quarter. That is a company locking down the one input that could bottleneck its GPU deployments: the optical interconnect. Returns across this sector have been historic over the past twelve months. But separate the revenue growers from the narrative trades. Some of these companies are printing real quarterly numbers that would impress in any sector. Others are carrying multi billion dollar market caps on sub $100 million in annual revenue. Same sector, wildly different risk. Every generation of AI infrastructure from here forward needs more photonics. Not less. The copper to light transition inside data centers is early. Co packaged optics is barely in deployment, and 1.6T transceivers are ramping with 3.2T already on roadmaps. The chain locks together: stress on any single link reprices every link above it."}}}, "rest_id": "2062502220889837836", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "263516", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2062636370233831773", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "963", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

10) SUBSTRATE / WAFER
The base wafers everything is grown on — InP/GaAs for lasers, SOI for silicon photonics.

$AXTI · $SLOIF (Soitec) · JP: Sumitomo, JX, Shin-Etsu, SUMCO · DE: Freiberger

7) InP LASER / EML
The light source. Silicon can't emit light — III-V indium phosphide does the lasing. The hardest physical piece.

$COHR (owns its InP fab) · $LITE · $AAOI · EU: Sivers, EFFECT Photonics · JP: Sumitomo, Mitsubishi · TW: LandMark

6) SiPho OPTICAL ENGINE (PIC)
Silicon-photonics chip integrating modulators/waveguides — the heart of a modern module / CPO.

$CRDO (DustPhotonics) · $COHR · $POET · Intel/Jabil · Lightmatter, Ayar (private)

text:
$STM at a new all-time high after hours. 🥂

The craziest part is this hasn’t hit the mainstream yet. 

STM is quietly ramping high-volume production of its PIC100 silicon photonics platform (800G and 1.6T optical transceivers) for hyperscaler AI clusters, with plans to quadruple capacity by 2027￼ backed by long-term customer reservations. 

The anchor underneath it all is a multi-year, multi-billion dollar AWS engagement ￼spanning connectivity, microcontrollers, and power ICs, which even came with warrants to AWS for up to 24.8 million ST shares ￼ that vest as AWS buys product.

 Top After-Hours Losers:  $JZ (Jianzhi Education) — -22.86%

$MYND (https://t.co/azHZg4Qaxk) — -17.17%

Key Takeaway:
Today’s after-hours action was dominated by micro-cap and small-cap names with very low floats, leading to exaggerated percentage moves. $XOS stands out with an incredible 132% surge — likely driven by major news or contract announcement.  These kinds of moves are high-risk, high-reward and often reverse quickly at the open. Always check volume, catalysts, and news before jumping in.  Trading Advice:  Use limit orders and tight risk management  
Many of these names have extremely low liquidity  
Watch for continuation or reversal in pre-market tomorrow

text: @ByrneHobart Photonics have already been discovered

حفلة اليوم برعاية سهم $LASE 🔥🔥

Laser Photonics Corporation

▪️ارتفاع ( 160% في يوم واحد ) 🚀

▪️الأسباب الرئيسية لانفجار السهم:

✅ تم اختيار نظام مكافحة الدرونز ضمن برنامج حكومي دفاعي ( أهم خبر ) 🔥
✅ شراء عضو مجلس إدارة من السوق المفتوح قبل شهر 32,000 سهم
✅ طلبية من Johnson & Johnson 
✅ قيمة سوقية صغيرة جداً (~80 مليون دولار)

❌ لا يوجد حتى الآن عقد رسمي من وزارة الدفاع
❌ طلبية J&J صغيرة نسبياً (250 ألف دولار فقط)

▪️نشاطها : 
- أنظمة الليزر الصناعية
- إزالة الصدأ والتنظيف بالليزر
- أنظمة دفاعية مضادة للدرونز (LSAD)
- تطبيقات طبية عبر شركة CMS Laser التابعة لها

▪️الخلاصة : 

السوق لا يسعر ما حدث اليوم فقط…
بل احتمال أن تتحول شركة صغيرة قيمتها 80 مليون دولار إلى شركة دفاعية تمتلك عقوداً حكومية مستقبلاً

text:
水曜朝ニュース（6/3 JST）

AIデータセンター、GPUだけ見てると次を取り逃がしそう。

$NVDA はVera Rubinで tokens per MW を前面に。$MRVL は「次のボトルネックはconnectivity」と整理。NVIDIAもSpectrum-X Ethernet Photonicsで電力効率5倍を打ち出した。

TrendForceは2027年HBM4契約価格の大幅上昇観測。$MU には追い風。

次は光I/O、HBM、電力・冷却、先進パッケージング。$LITE $COHR $VRT $TSM も見る。

text:
마벨(Marvell) CEO, "구리의 벽(Copper Wall)이 랙 내부로 이동하고 있으며, 이를 해결할 유일한 방법은 공동 패키징 광학(CPO)뿐"이라고 밝혀

*마벨 테크놀로지(Marvell Technology)의 CEO 맷 머피(Matt Murphy)는 "AI 인프라의 다음 주요 병목 현상은 연산(Compute)이나 메모리가 아니라, 바로 연결성(Connectivity)

*머피 CEO는 구리(Copper)에서 광학(Optical) 인터커넥트로의 전환이 먼 미래가 아닌 '지금 당장' 일어나고 있으며, 이는 반도체 산업 역사상 전례가 없을 정도로 거대한 수요의 물결을 일으킬 것이라고 말했음.

*구리의 벽(Copper Wall)의 물리학

- 신호가 구리 케이블을 통해 이동할 수 있는 거리는 대역폭에 반비례

- 레인당 100Gbps 수준: 구리 케이블은 약 5미터까지 도달할 수 있어 하나의 랙(Rack) 내부에서 서버들을 연결하기에 충분.

- ​레인당 200Gbps 수준: 그 거리는 약 2.5미터로 줄어들며, 이는 랙의 높이와 내부 라우팅 요구 사항을 감안할 때 딱 한계치에 해당.

- ​레인당 400Gbps 수준: 현재 업계가 나아가고 있는 이 단계에서는 구리선으로 랙 내부를 완전히 연결하는 것이 더 이상 불가능.

*구리의 벽이 한 단계 이동할 때마다, 광학으로 전환해야 하는 연결의 수는 최소 10배 이상 증가. 머피 CEO는 이를 광학 산업의 '수요 폭발'로 묘사.

*​공동 패키징 광학(CPO): 왜 필요한가?

- 랙 내부에서의 전환은 랙과 랙 사이(Inter-rack)에 사용되는 기존의 광학 기술로는 해결할 수 없다. 랙 내부의 연결 수는 랙 간 연결 수보다 약 10배 더 많음. 이 정도의 밀도에서 표준 광학 모듈을 장착하는 것은 전력과 공간 부족으로 인해 물리적으로 불가능.

- ​CPO는 광 엔진(Optical Engines)을 스위치나 연산 패키지에 직접 통합함으로써 이 문제를 해결. 광섬유를 전면 패널이 아닌 칩(Chip)으로 직접 가져오는 방식. 이 기술은 최첨단 CMOS 공정, 실리콘 포토닉스(SiPh), 첨단 패키징, 그리고 광 인터커넥트를 하나의 긴밀하게 통합된 시스템으로 결합.

- 엔비디아(Nvidia)의 베라 루빈(Vera Rubin) 플랫폼이 이미 생산 단계의 첫 CPO 기반 스위치인 '스펙트럼-X 이더넷 포토닉스(Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics)'를 도입했다는 점을 언급하며, 이러한 전환이 더 이상 이론에 머물지 않는다는 증거로 제시.

*오늘날의 AI 서버는 구리를 통한 고대역폭 통신을 위해 모든 부품이 서로 가까이 위치해야 하므로 CPU, GPU, 맞춤형 가속기, 메모리, 네트워크 인터페이스가 고정된 구성으로 묶여 있음. 이로 인해 연산 장치 대 메모리의 비율이 배포 시점에 고정되어, 어떤 작업(Workload)이 주어지든 시스템의 일부 자원은 항상 유휴 상태(Underutilized)로 남게 됨.

*​그러나 서버 내부의 연결이 광학으로 바뀌면 거리가 더 이상 아키처를 제약하지 않음. 연산, 메모리, 네트워킹을 각각 독립된 풀(Pool)로 분리(Disaggregated)한 뒤, 각 작업이 실제로 필요로 하는 바에 따라 동적으로 구성(Composed)할 수 있게 됨.

*머피 CEO는 "사상 처음으로 설계자들은 인터커넥트(연결)의 한계가 아니라, 오직 모델의 필요에 맞춰 AI 시스템을 설계할 수 있게 될 것입니다"라고 강조.

https://t.co/qMKbgEzsSR

#AI #Photonics #Materials

Vad jag vet har Sivers aldrig haft något PM med GF inom Photonics. Så egentligen är det första gången det blir officiellt. Men så klar,  kunde man haft med en notis och att man varit ecosystem partners på olika sätt. Dock är det nog nedan OCI MSA partners (som inte heller nämn), som egentligen gör nyheten extra intressant.

text:
Two Deep-Tech Innovators Presenting June 10: BIO-key International and Syntec Optics

Two very different technologies — biometric identity authentication and precision photonics — united by one theme: solving problems that require extreme accuracy in high-stakes environments.

BIO-key International (OTC: $BKYI) | 3:00 PM ET - A global leader in Identity and Access Management (IAM) and biometric authentication, serving defense, financial, and government customers across multiple continents.

Syntec Optics Holdings (Nasdaq: $OPTX) | 3:30 PM ET - One of the largest custom optics and photonics manufacturers in the United States, operating for over two decades out of its Rochester, NY facility.

Two companies. Two distinct markets. Both operating at the leading edge of their fields.

June 10, 2026 | Register free: https://t.co/F6isjDz8Ci

text:
🚨 Photonics may become the most important technology shift of the next decade.

Not AI software.

Not apps.

Not even GPUs alone.

The REAL revolution may be the replacement of electricity with LIGHT inside computing infrastructure. 🧵

Silicon electronics are approaching physical limits.

Modern AI infrastructure now faces: ⚠️ bandwidth ceilings ⚠️ power-consumption explosions ⚠️ heat-density problems ⚠️ latency bottlenecks ⚠️ interconnect saturation

The current architecture is becoming unsustainable.

This is where photonics enters.

Photonics = using LIGHT (photons) instead of electricity (electrons) to: 💡 transmit data 💡 process signals 💡 move information 💡 scale compute infrastructure

The implications are enormous.

We believe photonics is no longer speculative science fiction.

It is increasingly becoming: ⚠️ necessary ⚠️ commercially viable ⚠️ strategically essential

Especially for hyperscale AI systems.

The AI boom itself is creating the problem photonics solves.

As models scale: 📈 compute demand explodes 📈 rack density rises 📈 cooling loads surge 📈 electricity consumption skyrockets

Copper interconnects increasingly become the bottleneck.

Traditional electrical systems waste massive amounts of energy as heat.

Photonics changes the equation: ⚡ lower power consumption ⚡ faster transmission ⚡ lower latency ⚡ higher bandwidth ⚡ less thermal stress

This is why hyperscalers are aggressively pursuing it.

In this case the sequence is straightforward:

Step 1: 🤖 AI models scale exponentially

Step 2: ⚠️ Electrical interconnects hit limits

Step 3: 🔥 Cooling and power costs explode

Step 4: 💡 Photonics becomes economically necessary

Step 5: 🏗 Entire data-center architecture changes

This transition is already happening quietly.

Over the next 3–5 years: ⚠️ co-packaged optics ⚠️ optical switching ⚠️ silicon photonics ⚠️ photonic networking

…will increasingly replace traditional copper infrastructure.

The implications go far beyond performance.

Photonics may fundamentally reshape: 🏭 semiconductor design ⚡ power consumption 💧 cooling infrastructure 🧠 AI deployment economics 🏢 hyperscale construction

We believe this becomes one of the clearest examples of K-shaped technological consolidation.

Why?

Because only the largest players can afford: 💰 photonic R&D 💰 custom AI infrastructure 💰 advanced packaging 💰 optical integration (Watch chip makers rotating, new partnerships)

Smaller competitors increasingly get locked out.

The photonics transition favors: ✅ hyperscalers ✅ mega-cap semiconductor firms ✅ defense-linked infrastructure players ✅ vertically integrated ecosystems

The technological moat widens.

🚨 INVESTABLE PLAYERS

Several companies already sit directly inside this transition.

⚡ Broadcom ($AVGO) ⚡ Coherent ($COHR) ⚡ Lumentum ($LITE) ⚡ Intel ($INTC) ⚡ Laser Photonics ($LASE)

Each attacks a different layer of the stack.

$AVGO is one of the strongest large-cap photonics plays.

Broadcom already dominates: 🔌 networking 🔌 optical connectivity 🔌 silicon-photonics infrastructure 🔌 AI data-center interconnects

It sits directly at the AI bandwidth bottleneck.

$COHR is another critical player.

Coherent controls: 💡 industrial lasers 💡 optical subsystems 💡 photonic components 💡 semiconductor manufacturing optics

As photonic infrastructure scales, laser demand scales with it.

$LITE remains deeply leveraged to: 📡 fiber optics 📡 telecom photonics 📡 optical sensing 📡 AI networking expansion

The company effectively monetizes bandwidth growth itself.

$INTC is quietly positioning aggressively in silicon photonics.

Intel understands a critical reality:

Traditional scaling alone is no longer enough.

Future compute increasingly depends on: 💡 optical movement of data rather than purely electrical scaling.

Smaller pure-play names like: ⚡ $LASE ⚡ Ayar Labs (private) ⚡ Lightmatter (private)

represent the more speculative frontier.

Higher risk. Potentially enormous upside.

Especially if optical interconnect adoption accelerates faster than expected.

Emphasis

In photonics the bottlenecks are: ⚠️ optical integration ⚠️ laser systems ⚠️ advanced packaging ⚠️ thermal efficiency ⚠️ networking infrastructure

The defense angle matters enormously too.

Photonics is not just about AI chatbots.

It increasingly affects: 🛰 satellite systems 🎯 targeting infrastructure 📡 radar systems ⚔ electronic warfare 🤖 autonomous drones

This is becoming strategic infrastructure.

Another major theme:

Photonics helps reduce energy intensity.

This becomes critical because hyperscale AI now collides directly with: ⚡ grid shortages 💧 water scarcity 🔥 cooling constraints 🛢 diesel stress

The physical economy increasingly constrains digital expansion.

We have repeatedly emphasized:

The future AI race may not simply be about: “Who has the best model?”

It may become: “Who can physically power and cool computation?”

Photonics helps solve that problem.

This is why the market increasingly rewards: 🏗 infrastructure ⚡ utilities 💧 cooling 🧲 critical minerals 💡 photonic efficiency

The AI story is becoming deeply industrial.

The broader implication is profound:

Photonics may ultimately do to traditional electronics… …what the internet did to analog communication.

The infrastructure layer itself changes.

But the transition also accelerates concentration.

Only a narrowing circle of: 🏢 hyperscalers 🏦 mega-cap firms 🏭 industrial suppliers ⚔ defense-linked infrastructure players

…may fully benefit.

The K-shape deepens.

The irony is striking:

The AI revolution was originally marketed as democratizing technology.

Instead it increasingly centralizes: ⚡ compute ⚡ infrastructure ⚡ capital ⚡ energy access ⚡ photonic capability

into fewer hands.

We believe the market still underestimates how quickly photonics may become unavoidable.

Because the problem is no longer theoretical.

The electrical system itself is hitting physical limits.

The final takeaway:

The next generation of AI winners may not simply be software companies.

They may be the firms controlling: 💡 light transmission ⚡ power efficiency 🔥 thermal management 🏗 optical infrastructure

The future of computing increasingly belongs to photonics.

text:
============================================================
SOLIDIFICATION OF LIGHT AND PROGRAMMABLE MATTER
Connection Brief
============================================================

Solidification of Light:
- Refers to experiments making photons behave like condensed matter.
- Key breakthrough (March 2025): Italian researchers created the first
  "photonic supersolid" — light that has a rigid crystalline structure
  (solid-like) while flowing without friction (superfluid-like).
- Achieved using exciton-polaritons in semiconductor nanostructures.

Programmable Matter:
- Materials that can change physical properties (shape, density,
  optical traits, stiffness) in a controlled/programmable way.
- Includes metamaterials, shape-changing systems, and modular robotics.

Connections:
1. Light as a programmable medium: Supersolid light and polariton
   systems allow dynamic, reconfigurable quantum states controlled
   by lasers or external fields.

2. Programmable photonics overlap: Solid-light phenomena can serve
   as building blocks for adaptive optical chips, tunable lasers,
   optical neural networks, and quantum simulators.

3. Hybrid future tech: Combines exotic quantum behavior of "solid"
   light with the reconfigurability of smart materials, enabling
   potential applications in quantum computing, adaptive optics,
   and light-based programmable devices.

Current status: Mostly laboratory-scale quantum experiments, but
strong conceptual bridge toward future smart photonic materials.

References:
[1] Live Science / Nature (2025) - Photonic supersolid creation
[2] Wikipedia / ScienceAlert - Solid light and supersolids
[3] Wikipedia - Programmable matter
[4] Related works on polaritons and programmable photonics

text:
Researchers at Monash University have developed a tiny chip that could help pave the way for computers that process information using light instead of electricity. Published in the journal Nature Photonics, the study demonstrates a nanoscale device capable of generating, controlling and reading light-based signals on a single platform. The breakthrough uses an emerging technology called valleytronics, which relies on quantum properties in advanced materials to encode information. Researchers say the innovation could lead to faster computing, lower energy consumption and more efficient communication systems in the future.

Investigadores de la Universidad Monash desarrollaron un diminuto chip que podría ayudar a abrir el camino hacia computadoras capaces de procesar información utilizando luz en lugar de electricidad. Publicado en la revista Nature Photonics, el estudio demuestra un dispositivo a escala nanométrica capaz de generar, controlar y leer señales basadas en luz en una sola plataforma. El avance utiliza una tecnología emergente conocida como valleytrónica, que aprovecha propiedades cuánticas de materiales avanzados para codificar información. Los investigadores afirman que la innovación podría conducir a una computación más rápida, un menor consumo de energía y sistemas de comunicación más eficientes en el futuro.

text:
Karnataka CM @DKShivakumar on Friday met a high-level delegation from the German state of Thuringia, led by Minister-President Mario Voigt, to discuss strengthening economic ties and investment opportunities. 

Heavy Industries Minister @MBPatil said Karnataka and Thuringia can collaborate in optics, photonics, semiconductors, biotech, EV manufacturing, renewable energy and advanced manufacturing. Shivakumar highlighted opportunities in technology transfer, innovation, skill exchange and next-generation technologies. Voigt praised Karnataka’s R&D ecosystem, startup network, engineering talent, quantum technology & aerospace infrastructure.

Photonics bottlenecks go way beyond lasers. 

CPO/NPO integration, thermal mgmt, and OCS switches are key scalers. Diversify across the chain for AI cluster exposure. 

Pluggables probably start ramping in 2027, CPO follows. High TAM but watch yields, hyperscaler quals & execution risks. 

Picks & shovels win regardless.

text:
While Nasdaq is down 3%, the number one gainer on the S&P today is FedEx Freight $FDXF at +9%.

But, no one cared about this 5 days ago, because it's not related to bottlenecks in photonics.

Yet, trucking - especially LTL - is a bottleneck. Plus, the re-rating vs. $ODFL, $SAIA, and $XPO after spin.

Though, it's too boring for X, and such posts are becoming increasingly a waste of time.

This was #1 reason I've kept $FDX but sold my $UPS couple years ago. The theoretical value of the distributed stake implied an initial $FDXF price in the mid-to-high $170s range. As typical with spin-offs, they get sold and right now we're $150s. Estimated FY2026 revenue: $8.7B (largest North American LTL carrier by revenue). It carries major debt due to a $4.1B cash dividend paid to FedEx pre-spin. Old Dominion $ODFL: EV/EBITDA 25–27x, PE 43–47x. Superior operating ratio 72–75% range historically/recently and consistent execution. Saia $SAIA and $XPO: Also premium high 40+ PE, strong EV/EBITDA, with growth/margin stories. Potential $FDFX catalysts: - Immediate S&P 500 and Dow Jones Transportation Average inclusion (index buying). - Pure-play LTL focus vs. being buried inside $FDX . - Potential for margin/ROIC improvement under dedicated management and capex/IT focus. -Long-term re-rating opportunity if it narrows the efficiency gap toward $ODFL $SAIA levels. I added 50 shares simply because the 50 I got is too small. Similar to a semiconductor IPO, hard to think of a better time in recent history to do a trucking IPO/spinoff."}}}, "rest_id": "2061539530730254635", "source": "Twitter Web Client", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "14451", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2062943437528404282", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "10010", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
PhotonVentures is a European venture capital fund dedicated to integrated photonics, investing in companies from seed to Series A and supporting their transition from early validation to scalable businesses. As a specialized deep tech investor, the fund combines capital with direct access to a global ecosystem of foundries, designers, and system integrators via PhotonDelta.

This article outlines how investors evaluate early-stage photonic chip startups, what differentiates investment-ready teams, and how companies can position themselves for scale, drawing on the perspective of PhotonVentures. It focuses on the practical signals investors look for across technology, market readiness, and ecosystem alignment.

Full interview here: https://t.co/HKQNA1CSJP

#engineering #technology #photonics #PICs

@photondelta

4007}, {"from_index": 4787, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4792}, {"from_index": 4831, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4847}]}, "text": "Copper can't keep up with AI. That's not an opinion, it's physics. Every data center being built right now is replacing electrical connections with light. NVIDIA confirmed it with $4.5 billion in direct investment. I mapped 25 public companies across the photonics value chain: Every AI cluster being built today hits the same wall. A hundred thousand GPUs mean nothing if the data can't move between them fast enough. Copper maxed out years ago and photonics replaced it: lasers, optical fiber, and transceivers that push data at the speed of light. The AI transceiver market doubled in two years. NVIDIA committed $4.5 billion across three photonics companies this year alone. This is where the infrastructure money is going. Here's the full value chain: 🔬 MATERIALS & WAFERS This is the bottom of the chain. Every laser and transceiver starts as a wafer substrate: indium phosphide, gallium arsenide, germanium, specialty glass. Nobody above this layer can produce anything without these inputs, and right now the most critical one, indium phosphide, is the tightest material in the entire AI supply chain. The gap between demand and capacity is getting worse, not better. I think this is the most asymmetric layer on the map. Investors chase the transceiver companies and ignore who grows the substrates underneath them. But NVIDIA is writing checks worth billions in cash and warrants to lock up supply from this exact layer. First link in the chain, last to get attention, and the one that chokes everything above it if it breaks. Tickers: $GLW, $AXTI, $IQE, $AIXA, $AMS 💡 CORE PHOTONIC DEVICES This layer converts electricity into light and back. Without it, zero data moves through fiber. NVIDIA dropped $4 billion into two companies here this year just to secure production capacity, and both of them joined the S&P 500 within weeks of each other. That should tell you how fast this went from niche to essential. The supply gap is not closing. The companies shipping next gen lasers at volume can be counted on one hand, and switching suppliers takes years of requalification. Order books stretch past twelve months. Every next generation GPU cluster consumes more of these components than the last, and no one can substitute them on short notice. I watch this layer more closely than any other. Tickers: $IPGP, $COHR, $LITE, $LASR, $SIVE 🔌 COMPONENTS & MODULES The companies here take raw lasers and detectors, package them into finished transceivers and modules, and ship them straight to hyperscalers. If the layers below are the engine, this is the vehicle that actually reaches the customer. Hyperscaler purchase orders land here. The revenue acceleration shows up here first. What I like about this layer is that you can underwrite it today, not in two years. These are businesses with signed capacity commitments and product already moving. The consolidation angle matters too: larger photonics players have already started absorbing standalone module companies, and whoever remains independent gains pricing power as options thin out. Tickers: $AAOI, $MTSI, $VIAV, $LPTH ⚙️ SYSTEMS & EQUIPMENT No company above this layer can manufacture a single photonic component without the machines built here. One of these names holds 100% of the EUV lithography market with zero competitors. Others supply the bonding equipment for co packaged optics or the process control instruments used across the majority of advanced packaging lines. If photonics is the gold rush, this is the layer selling the picks. My honest take: this is where the smart, patient capital parks. Equipment companies have pricing power and multi year order books that generate cash through full capex cycles. They attract holders who don't panic on the first pullback. The stocks don't run 1,000% overnight, but they compound while everything above them swings, and that tradeoff is worth more than most people give it credit for. Tickers: $ASML, $BESI, $ASM, $LPKF, $MKSI 🔍 TEST, METROLOGY & YIELD The most ignored layer on this map, and arguably the one with the cleanest business model. Every wafer, laser, and transceiver has to be tested and verified before it ships. As speeds climb and photonic devices get more complex, the testing challenge compounds fast. The industry is now constrained not only by what it can build but by what it can prove actually works. Yield is money. Better defect detection means better margins for every company upstream, which is why foundries keep buying test equipment even when they slash budgets everywhere else. These are capital light businesses tied to every unit of production across the chain. Last check before product hits the customer, and one of the few layers where demand doesn't cycle down when the rest of semis softens. Tickers: $CAMT, $FORM, $AEHR, $ONTO, $VIAV 🧠FINAL THOUGHTS The NVIDIA capital concentration tells the whole story. One company wrote $4.5 billion in checks to three photonics suppliers in a single quarter. That is a company locking down the one input that could bottleneck its GPU deployments: the optical interconnect. Returns across this sector have been historic over the past twelve months. But separate the revenue growers from the narrative trades. Some of these companies are printing real quarterly numbers that would impress in any sector. Others are carrying multi billion dollar market caps on sub $100 million in annual revenue. Same sector, wildly different risk. Every generation of AI infrastructure from here forward needs more photonics. Not less. The copper to light transition inside data centers is early. Co packaged optics is barely in deployment, and 1.6T transceivers are ramping with 3.2T already on roadmaps. The chain locks together: stress on any single link reprices every link above it."}}}, "rest_id": "2062502220889837836", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "263516", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2062936149908213920", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "38", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
📊 Top stocks on LunarCrush right now: who's moving and why.

These are ranked by LunarCrush AltRank, which measures social attention and market performance across the entire stock market. The data breaks into three groups today. 

1️⃣ AI, space, and defense names with real catalysts 
2️⃣ Biotech and health plays moving on deals and data 
3️⃣ A name-mixup trap inflating one struggling stock

Reddit $RDDT +8.92% 
Sits at #1. People are talking up its AI data-licensing deals as a real money source, pushing its share of the stock conversation to almost 5x its normal level. Sentiment is only 62% though, with analyst downgrades pushing back.

GRAIL $GRAL +10.15% 
The cancer-screening company caught a bid on partnership rumors ahead of new trial data. One caveat: a lot of the "Grail" chatter is actually about fashion resale and Pokemon "grails," so the real signal is smaller than the number looks.

Alight $ALIT +8.38% 
Be careful with this one. Almost all the buzz is about Alight Motion, a video-editing app, not the actual HR company. The stock itself is down on the week with delisting rumors hanging over it, so the high rank is mostly a name mix-up.

Abivax $ABVX +16.72% 
This biotech popped on takeover rumors with key trial data due in Q2. Mentions and engagement hit all-time highs, but the move is speculation-driven, and fears of the company raising money are capping how far bulls will chase it.

Redwire $RDW +17.59% 
A space and defense company riding SpaceX IPO excitement and a growing pile of military contracts. It's up about 129% on the month at 86% sentiment, though some worry about it issuing new shares.

Applied Optoelectronics $AAOI +11.81% 
The clearest AI-hardware story on the board. It makes the optical parts data centers need, and traders see a real demand "supercycle" as big cloud players ramp orders. Strong 84% sentiment.

Laser Photonics $LASE +9.74% 
The wild card. It's up over 300% in a week after the U.S. military validated its anti-drone laser system, and trading volume exploded about 30x. But it's a tiny company, so expect sharp swings both ways.

text:
The recent buzz surrounding Marvell Technology $MRVL becoming a potential trillion-dollar company stems largely from a high-profile endorsement by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at the Computex 2026 trade show. The analysts and industry observers point to several fundamental pillars that support this ambitious valuation thesis:
1 Essential Data Center Connectivity: As AI workloads become disaggregated and distributed across thousands of chips, connectivity has become the primary bottleneck. Marvell provides the essential networking and connectivity chips that allow these massive clusters to communicate at the high speeds required for modern AI.  

2 Strategic Nvidia Partnership: Nvidia, which has a deep interest in the success of the AI ecosystem, recently invested $2 billion in $MRVL. This partnership, centered on NVLink Fusion, aims to integrate their technologies to build more efficient AI infrastructure, essentially making Marvell a critical partner rather than just a vendor.  

3 Leadership in Custom Silicon (ASICs): Marvell is a leader in custom-designed chips for major hyperscalers like Amazon and Microsoft. Unlike general-purpose GPUs, these ASICs are tailored for specific tasks, creating sticky, multi-year revenue streams that provide a massive, predictable growth foundation.  

4 Optical Networking Expertise: Marvell is at the forefront of the shift toward optical interconnects, which use light instead of electricity to move data. This technology is critical for scaling AI performance as bandwidth demands continue to explode.  

5 Industry-Leading Product Innovation: The company recently launched the Teralynx T100, an industry-first 102.4 Tbps switch designed specifically to address latency and energy constraints in AI data centers, keeping them ahead of competitors.  

6 Rapidly Expanding AI Infrastructure Revenue: Data center operations now drive roughly 74% of Marvell's total revenue. The company has projected strong, sustained double-digit growth in this segment, fueled by the global buildout of AI data centers.  

7 Aggressive M&A Strategy: Marvell has been strategically acquiring startups like Celestial AI and XConn to bolster its capabilities in advanced packaging and optical interconnects, allowing it to quickly fill gaps in its technological roadmap.  

8 Silicon Photonics Potential: Marvell is heavily investing in silicon photonics, a technology that promises to significantly improve the energy efficiency and data transfer speeds of future AI clusters. This is widely considered a "real" long-term growth driver.

9 One-Stop Shop Positioning: By offering a comprehensive suite of solutions, from processors and ASICs to optical I/O and switching. Marvell is positioning itself as a vital, end-to-end infrastructure provider for the cloud-scale AI era.  

10 Market Momentum and Valuation Re-rating: Following Jensen Huang's endorsement, the market has begun to aggressively re-price Marvell, reflecting the expectation that it will capture a massive share of the AI hardware market in the coming years. This investor confidence acts as a catalyst for the capital influx needed to support a larger market cap.

text:
$MRVL Marvell Technology: Architect of the AI Connectivity Backbone. Investment Thesis.

Marvell Technology has completed a strategic transformation that few legacy semiconductor companies execute cleanly. The pivot away from traditional networking and storage semiconductors toward custom silicon and high-speed connectivity for AI data center infrastructure is not a repositioning in progress — it's largely done, and the financial trajectory is beginning to reflect it. The Celestial AI acquisition extends that positioning into optical interconnects and photonic fabrics, addressing the next layer of AI infrastructure bottleneck where copper interconnects hit physical limits at scale.

The hyperscaler demand dynamic is the engine behind the revenue ramp thesis. Custom ASIC design wins with major cloud providers represent a different category of customer relationship than merchant silicon sales — they create deep technical integration, long program lifecycles, and switching costs that are measured in years of co-development rather than procurement cycles. That stickiness is a genuine competitive moat, but it also concentrates revenue in ways that make the business sensitive to program-level decisions at a small number of accounts.

Matthew Murphy's leadership has been the consistent thread through the transformation. The strategic decisions — which legacy businesses to exit, which adjacencies to pursue, which acquisitions to make — reflect a coherent vision that has been executed with unusual discipline for a company managing simultaneous portfolio transitions. That track record matters when evaluating whether the optical interconnect bet will be executed with similar precision.

Technical execution at advanced nodes is the capability risk that the strategic narrative doesn't eliminate. Custom silicon at leading-edge process nodes requires sustained investment in design talent, EDA infrastructure, and customer co-development resources. The competitive environment for that talent — against hyperscalers building internal silicon teams and peers targeting the same design win opportunities — is intensifying, which adds cost pressure alongside the execution complexity.

The valuation is the honest tension in the thesis. Marvell is priced as a confirmed AI infrastructure compounder, and the revenue ramp forecasts embedded in the current multiple require hyperscaler demand to remain elevated, program ramps to execute on schedule, and optical interconnect adoption to accelerate at the pace the Celestial AI acquisition assumes. Each of those is plausible individually — together, they represent a high bar that leaves the stock exposed to any single point of execution friction. The strategic positioning is among the strongest in the AI semiconductor supply chain; the question is how much of that quality is already in the price.
