Investment Thesis. Marvell Technology has completed a strategic transformation that few legacy semiconductor companies execute cleanly. The pivot away from traditional networking and storage semiconductors toward custom silicon and high-speed connectivity for AI data center infrastructure is not a repositioning in progress — it's largely done, and the financial trajectory is beginning to reflect it. The Celestial AI acquisition extends that positioning into optical interconnects and photonic fabrics, addressing the next layer of AI infrastructure bottleneck where copper interconnects hit physical limits at scale. The hyperscaler demand dynamic is the engine behind the revenue ramp thesis. Custom ASIC design wins with major cloud providers represent a different category of customer relationship than merchant silicon sales — they create deep technical integration, long program lifecycles, and switching costs that are measured in years of co-development rather than procurement cycles. That stickiness is a genuine competitive moat, but it also concentrates revenue in ways that make the business sensitive to program-level decisions at a small number of accounts. Matthew Murphy's leadership has been the consistent thread through the transformation. The strategic decisions — which legacy businesses to exit, which adjacencies to pursue, which acquisitions to make — reflect a coherent vision that has been executed with unusual discipline for a company managing simultaneous portfolio transitions. That track record matters when evaluating whether the optical interconnect bet will be executed with similar precision. Technical execution at advanced nodes is the capability risk that the strategic narrative doesn't eliminate. Custom silicon at leading-edge process nodes requires sustained investment in design talent, EDA infrastructure, and customer co-development resources. The competitive environment for that talent — against hyperscalers building internal silicon teams and peers targeting the same design win opportunities — is intensifying, which adds cost pressure alongside the execution complexity. The valuation is the honest tension in the thesis. Marvell is priced as a confirmed AI infrastructure compounder, and the revenue ramp forecasts embedded in the current multiple require hyperscaler demand to remain elevated, program ramps to execute on schedule, and optical interconnect adoption to accelerate at the pace the Celestial AI acquisition assumes. Each of those is plausible individually — together, they represent a high bar that leaves the stock exposed to any single point of execution friction. The strategic positioning is among the strongest in the AI semiconductor supply chain; the question is how much of that quality is already in the price."}}}, "quoted_status_result": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "article": {"article_results": {"result": {"cover_media": {"id": "QXBpTWVkaWE6DAAFCgABHJ/ZrKgWgAAKAAIZgoybDlbAAAAA", "media_id": "2062606489936625664", "media_info": {"__typename": "ApiImage", "color_info": {"palette": [{"percentage": 88.28, "rgb": {"blue": 254, "green": 254, "red": 254}}, {"percentage": 9.33, "rgb": {"blue": 43, "green": 44, "red": 42}}]}, "original_img_height": 778, "original_img_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HJ_ZrKgWgAAZhaU.jpg", "original_img_width": 1945}, "media_key": "3_2062606489936625664"}, "id": "QXJ0aWNsZUVudGl0eToyMDYyNjA1NDU4MDI4ODA2MTQ1", "lifecycle_state": {"modified_at_secs": 1780602097}, "metadata": {"first_published_at_secs": 1780602097}, "preview_text": "EXECUTIVE OVERVIEW: Marvell Technology is now one of the most direct public-market vehicles for the AI data-center connectivity bottleneck. text:
Here are 5 AI-related stocks I am looking at for 2026-7

AR WEARABLES & DISPLAY $KEEL 

Kopin Corporation. AI/AR Wearables Play. Bullish technical pattern crossing the 50-day MA. Direct catalyst with military contracts for lightweight display tech.

OPTICAL INTERCONNECT & TRANSCEIVERS $POET

POET Technologies. Optical Interconnect Leader. Essential silicon photonics components for next-gen data centers. Perfect play for the 'Memory & Optical' bottleneck. 

Applied Optoelectronics.  $AAOI
 
Optical transceivers for data center buildouts. Demand is high with strong manufacturing scalability. Low valuation compared to legacy optical plays.

RF, MILLIMETER WAVE & NETWORKING $SIVEF

Sivers Semiconductors. RF & Millimeter wave solutions. Crucial infrastructure for 5G/AI network architecture and satellite communications. Moving average crossover with strong volume.

AI CLOUD & GPU INFRASTRUCTURE $NEBIUS

Nebius Group. AI Cloud & GPU infrastructure. Emerging European player acting as a high-growth alternative to CoreWeave and Applied Digital. Proximity to key tech hubs gives them a massive edge.

text:
$CRDO Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Credo Tech

OVERVIEW

Credo operates as a hyper-growth high-speed connectivity semiconductor provider that has a broad portfolio of products spanning SerDes, optical DSPs, Active Electrical Cables (AECs), and silicon photonics solutions. FY2025 proved their explosive growth narrative, achieving an annual revenue of $1.34B, growing +206% YoY. The AI/HPC data center segment delivered the bulk of growth, driven by hyperscaler demand, AI cluster infrastructure, and optical/electrical interconnects (800G/1.6T).

$CRDO is the dominant force in delivering copper architecture through its market-leading AECs. Low-power, high-reliability copper cables that facilitate short-reach intra-rack and spine connections in AI clusters today. Over the coming years, the industry will transition toward photonics for longer-reach, higher-density interconnects at 1.6T+ speeds, where copper hits physical limits. The fear with this development was that Credo would be essentially phased out of the market with time, leading to the massive correction in early 2026.

However, the recent DustPhotonics acquisition (completed in May 2026) brings in-house silicon photonics PIC technology for 800G/1.6T/3.2T+ NPO and CPO, solidifying its future fully vertically integrated stack (SerDes + DSP + silicon photonics + system integration). This positions Credo perfectly for the next decade of AI infrastructure evolution, capturing copper today and optics tomorrow.

In my eyes, This company is a premier "picks-and-shovels" play on the connectivity buildout. Margins are disgustingly high (60%+ gross) now and in the years moving forward with a strong FCF + balance sheet, providing the company a leg-up on competition due to minimal capital intensity given their fabless model. These margins are expected to be sustained or even expand further as higher-ASP optical and silicon photonics products ramp.

--------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$CRDO has consistently outperformed expectations without sandbagging guidance. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI connectivity + photonics acceleration.

Credo maintains deep partnerships with all major hyperscalers (including $MSFT, $AMZN, $META, $ORCL and xAI), a fact known due to their famous "purple cables" acting as visual proof across imaged AI/HPC builds. Their solutions are currently powering xAI’s Colossus cluster via ZeroFlap AECs to maximize GPU utilization and cluster uptime. This is the part many sleep on! These existing relationships give Credo massive leverage to drive future growth when hyperscalers are primed to adopt the new 1.6T+ optical DSPs and silicon photonics products as the transition accelerates.

The AI connectivity segment projects strong growth of ~80%+ YoY in FY26 (with a re-inflection in this strength in the back half of 2027) due to expanded hyperscaler/AI focus. Over FY2026, the company is projected to report ~$2.4B Revenue and ~$6 EPS. I positioned before this inflection starts in March. I am thankful for the opportunity we were provided.

This growth fuels income statement expansion, with non-GAAP gross margins holding steady in the mid-to-high 60s% and operating margins nearing 45-50% as higher-margin optical and photonics products enter the mix further down the line, driving EPS sharply higher. The balance sheet is pristine, currently holding $1.4B in cash with strong FCF generation and zero debt thanks to the fabless model. Product roadmaps support their ambitious outlook, as shown by their unveiling of 1.6T optical DSPs and silicon photonics integration becoming available with next-generation AI programs such as 800G acceleration ramping in 2H 2026. 

--------------------------

FAIR VALUATION ESTIMATE

Using an aggregated/blended valuation approach based on many different disciplines of FA (DCF, Relative Multiples, Analyst Coverage, etcetera)...

FVE = $260/share

This does not take into account the potential for technical momentum to accelerate price far beyond this FVE due to future prospects. One of my favorite tidbits from an FA standpoint for this company is that their NTM P/E is 36.7 and PEG sits at 0.2. This is EXTREMELY LOW in my opinion for the growth promised. Tons of room higher from a valuation standpoint relative to other companies.

--------------------------

LONG-TERM ESTIMATES

Assuming $CRDO meets aggressive growth targets and achieves success in mixing CPO + NPO products into their portfolio with clear customer adoption, this is their potential path forward valuation-wise when using P/E multiples and expected EPS values.

EY2026 = $350+
EY2027 = $500+
EY2028 = $700+
EY2029 = $900+

One of my favorite longs at this time. There is risk involved regarding the integration of DustPhotonics post-acquisition, but I very much so trust management considering their track record.

Credit to @damnang2 for the image breaking down their product portfolio.

4672, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4679}, {"from_index": 4687, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4693}]}, "text": "$CRDO Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Credo Tech

OVERVIEW

Credo operates as a hyper-growth high-speed connectivity semiconductor provider that has a broad portfolio of products spanning SerDes, optical DSPs, Active Electrical Cables (AECs), and silicon photonics solutions. FY2025 proved their explosive growth narrative, achieving an annual revenue of $1.34B, growing +206% YoY. The AI/HPC data center segment delivered the bulk of growth, driven by hyperscaler demand, AI cluster infrastructure, and optical/electrical interconnects (800G/1.6T).

$CRDO is the dominant force in delivering copper architecture through its market-leading AECs. Low-power, high-reliability copper cables that facilitate short-reach intra-rack and spine connections in AI clusters today. Over the coming years, the industry will transition toward photonics for longer-reach, higher-density interconnects at 1.6T+ speeds, where copper hits physical limits. The fear with this development was that Credo would be essentially phased out of the market with time, leading to the massive correction in early 2026.

However, the recent DustPhotonics acquisition (completed in May 2026) brings in-house silicon photonics PIC technology for 800G/1.6T/3.2T+ NPO and CPO, solidifying its future fully vertically integrated stack (SerDes + DSP + silicon photonics + system integration). This positions Credo perfectly for the next decade of AI infrastructure evolution, capturing copper today and optics tomorrow.

In my eyes, This company is a premier "picks-and-shovels" play on the connectivity buildout. Margins are disgustingly high (60%+ gross) now and in the years moving forward with a strong FCF + balance sheet, providing the company a leg-up on competition due to minimal capital intensity given their fabless model. These margins are expected to be sustained or even expand further as higher-ASP optical and silicon photonics products ramp.

--------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$CRDO has consistently outperformed expectations without sandbagging guidance. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI connectivity + photonics acceleration.

Credo maintains deep partnerships with all major hyperscalers (including $MSFT, $AMZN, $META, $ORCL and xAI), a fact known due to their famous "purple cables" acting as visual proof across imaged AI/HPC builds. Their solutions are currently powering xAI’s Colossus cluster via ZeroFlap AECs to maximize GPU utilization and cluster uptime. This is the part many sleep on! These existing relationships give Credo massive leverage to drive future growth when hyperscalers are primed to adopt the new 1.6T+ optical DSPs and silicon photonics products as the transition accelerates.

The AI connectivity segment projects strong growth of ~80%+ YoY in FY26 (with a re-inflection in this strength in the back half of 2027) due to expanded hyperscaler/AI focus. Over FY2026, the company is projected to report ~$2.4B Revenue and ~$6 EPS. I positioned before this inflection starts in March. I am thankful for the opportunity we were provided.

This growth fuels income statement expansion, with non-GAAP gross margins holding steady in the mid-to-high 60s% and operating margins nearing 45-50% as higher-margin optical and photonics products enter the mix further down the line, driving EPS sharply higher. The balance sheet is pristine, currently holding $1.4B in cash with strong FCF generation and zero debt thanks to the fabless model. Product roadmaps support their ambitious outlook, as shown by their unveiling of 1.6T optical DSPs and silicon photonics integration becoming available with next-generation AI programs such as 800G acceleration ramping in 2H 2026. 

--------------------------

FAIR VALUATION ESTIMATE

Using an aggregated/blended valuation approach based on many different disciplines of FA (DCF, Relative Multiples, Analyst Coverage, etcetera)...

FVE = $260/share

This does not take into account the potential for technical momentum to accelerate price far beyond this FVE due to future prospects. One of my favorite tidbits from an FA standpoint for this company is that their NTM P/E is 36.7 and PEG sits at 0.2. This is EXTREMELY LOW in my opinion for the growth promised. Tons of room higher from a valuation standpoint relative to other companies.

--------------------------

LONG-TERM ESTIMATES

Assuming $CRDO meets aggressive growth targets and achieves success in mixing CPO + NPO products into their portfolio with clear customer adoption, this is their potential path forward valuation-wise when using P/E multiples and expected EPS values.

EY2026 = $350+
EY2027 = $500+
EY2028 = $700+
EY2029 = $900+

One of my favorite longs at this time. There is risk involved regarding the integration of DustPhotonics post-acquisition, but I very much so trust management considering their track record.

Credit to @damnang2 for the image breaking down their product portfolio."}}}, "rest_id": "2062621942885331091", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2353", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}
